Evidence summary: Safeguarding: Class B dedicated safeguarding/monitoring system. Real-time keystroke + screen monitoring on student devices, 24/7 human moderator team plus behavioural analysis, level 1-5 risk grading, and tiered escalation to DSLs (high risk by phone 'within minutes', medium by email, low to dashboard) with contextual screenshot evidence. Risk categories include self-harm/suicidal ideation, grooming/CSE, cyberbullying, radicalisation. Aligns to KCSIE/Prevent. https://smoothwall.com/solutions/monitor ; https://exa.net.uk/protect-connect/your-school/monitoring-smoothwall/ || DataPrivacy: Acts as data processor, customer is controller; UK GDPR (retained EU law) with UK representative Smoothwall Ltd. Public DPA incorporating EU SCCs (Annex 1) and UK IDTA (Annex 2); public named sub-processor list (24 named, 10-day change notice); encryption in transit and at rest; NIST Cybersecurity Framework; retention up to 15 months (18 for Pulse); deletion of personal data on termination; under-18 PII deleted when no longer needed. https://qoria.com/privacy ; https://qoria.com/legal/data-processing-addendum ; https://qoria.com/privacy/sub-processors || Age: N/A || Accessibility: Published accessibility statement 'Striving for compliance with WCAG 2.1 Level AA and beyond' with a Monitor-specific downloadable Accessibility Conformance Report (VPAT 2.5Rev edition), alongside ACRs for Filter, Classwize, Pulse and Online Safety Hub. https://qoria.com/accessibility-guidelines || Transparency: Discloses how Monitor works (keystroke + screen capture, behavioural technology grading risk, 24/7 human moderation removing false positives, tiered escalation), retention periods, encryption, NIST CSF, and a public named sub-processor list. https://smoothwall.com/solutions/monitor ; https://qoria.com/privacy/sub-processors ; https://qoria.com/legal/data-processing-addendum
Evidence gaps: No first-party Smoothwall page fetched gives an explicit current KCSIE 2025 / UK SIC accreditation statement for Monitor specifically (filtering is described as 'UK SIC accredited', monitoring is not on the page fetched). Case-management integration and record-integrity/audit-trail detail not verified from a fetched primary source. | No explicit reference to the ICO Children's Code (Age Appropriate Design Code). No explicit clause prohibiting use of pupil/personal data for AI/model training. Data residency 'not always practicable'; transfers outside jurisdiction including USA are possible. Children's Code conformance and training-use posture unverified. | Privacy policy references under-18 data handling but states no enforced product minimum age; not applicable to a staff-operated monitoring system rather than a pupil-facing product. | Stated target is WCAG 2.1 AA (not 2.2); 'striving for' rather than asserting full conformance. Explicit assistive-technology and SEND/low-literacy support not detailed on the statement page; ACR PDF contents not independently fetched. | No model/version disclosure for the 'advanced behavioural technology' (architecture, training, vendor). No published accuracy/false-positive/false-negative rates or formal hallucination/limitations posture beyond marketing claims of reducing false positives. No independent auditability evidence of detection performance.

Evidence summary: Safeguarding: Pupil-facing wellbeing chatbot. Safety page: "Woebot is trained to detect concerning language during a conversation. When it does, Woebot immediately offers people the option to view information about external resources for further help" but "Woebot is not a crisis service." A Safety Assessment Committee of clinical/regulatory experts reviews data; conversations are human-authored (https://woebothealth.com/safety/). Terms direct at-risk users to "DISCONTINUE USE... CALL 911" and state "USE OF THE SERVICES IS NOT FOR EMERGENCIES" (https://woebothealth.com/terms-webview/). || DataPrivacy: UK GDPR recognised: EEA/Switzerland/UK users can complain to the ICO; EU Standard Contractual Clauses used for transfers; right to erasure and in-app deletion offered; DPO at privacy@woebothealth.com (https://woebothealth.com/privacy-webview/). Conversation data used "to provide" and "to improve our Services," not shared with third parties for advertising. Encrypted at rest and TLS in transit; annual third-party penetration testing; HIPAA-aligned (https://woebothealth.com/security/). || Age: Terms: "You may use the Services only if you are at least 13 years of age"; under-18s require parent/guardian acceptance; under-13 data not knowingly collected (https://woebothealth.com/terms-webview/). Product included "content specific to teens, including media literacy, bullying in school settings, and body image" (MobiHealthNews). || Accessibility: Published accessibility statements for iOS and Android: "The Woebot iOS application is partially conformant with the WCAG 2.1 AA standard" (https://woebothealth.com/ios-accessibility-statement/). Independent audit by InterAccess (per search of accessibility pages). 13 issues documented including VoiceOver users unable to complete onboarding, unlabelled input fields, insufficient colour contrast. || Transparency: Safety page discloses the system "leverages natural language processing to interpret those inputs and identify which expert-crafted technique would be most beneficial," with conversations "written by conversational writers... clinical experts" (https://woebothealth.com/safety/). Responses were pre-scripted/rules-based rather than generative (STAT/MobiHealthNews). Studies reviewed by an IRB, protocols and results published openly.
Evidence gaps: No KCSIE alignment, no DSL/school-reporting escalation pathway, no supervision model for school deployment (designed for individual/parental oversight, not a safeguarding-led setting). Crisis guidance is US-centric (911). Product retired 30 June 2025 (https://woebothealth.com/faq/), so no current safeguarding support is deliverable. | All data hosted in the United States (AWS/GCP) — no UK/EU residency. No published DPA/data-processing terms for institutional buyers; retention is open-ended ("as long as you use our Services"); no named sub-processor list; no ICO Children's Code / Age Appropriate Design Code reference despite teen content. Product retired and account data anonymised as of 31 July 2025 (https://woebothealth.com/faq/). | 13+ floor excludes KS1/KS2 and lower KS3 (primary and Year 7-8 pupils). Age gate is self-declared with no robust enforcement. No mapping to UK key stages. No age-appropriate design assurance. Product no longer available. | Targets WCAG 2.1 AA, not 2.2; only PARTIAL conformance with significant known blockers (onboarding inaccessible to screen-reader users). Statement dated 27 January 2023 and not updated; no VPAT/ACR. No SEND or low-literacy provision documented. Product retired, so fixes will not be delivered. | No specific model name/version, no architecture detail, no hallucination posture (largely N/A given non-generative design but not stated as such for buyers), no auditability/logging detail for institutions. Transparency is research-oriented, not procurement-oriented.

Evidence summary: Safeguarding: 24/7 human Wall Guides monitor all member content; NLP/watchwords flag risk/crisis language but "all moderation decisions are made by Togetherall's team of human moderators, never by artificial intelligence or algorithms." Immediate escalation for crisis keywords (suicide, self-harm) and suicidal-assessment scores; named Clinical Safety Officer (Dr Tim Rogers); Wall Guides DBS-checked and separated from registration data; report mechanisms ("Ask a Wall Guide"/"Report post"). https://togetherall.com/en-gb/faqs/safety-and-privacy/ || DataPrivacy: Data controller Togetherall Limited (England & Wales, co. 06227377); compliance with Data Protection Legislation under Art.6/Art.9 lawful bases; data "stored and processed on Our secure servers located in the UK or the EEA"; encrypted at rest and in transit (HTTPS/TLS); 8-year retention post account closure then secure deletion; sub-processor list published; no mention of using personal data to train AI models. https://togetherall.com/en-gb/privacy-policy/ || Age: Privacy policy states "Our Services are available to individuals who are 16+ years old. We do not knowingly collect data relating to people under the age of 16." Anonymous peer-support model designed for older teens/adults. https://togetherall.com/en-gb/privacy-policy/ || Accessibility: Accessibility statement dated June 2025 states development follows "accessibility guidelines like WCAG and ISO 30071-1" and lists known issues "based on WCAG 2.2 AA standards"; tested with NVDA/Chrome (Windows) and VoiceOver/Safari (iOS); contact route provided. https://togetherall.com/en-gb/accessibility/ || Transparency: Public pages disclose that NLP and watchwords are used only to detect/flag risk and rule breaches, with all moderation and care decisions made by humans "never by artificial intelligence or algorithms"; named Clinical Safety Officer. https://togetherall.com/en-gb/faqs/safety-and-privacy/
Evidence gaps: No explicit KCSIE 2025 alignment statement; under-18 supervision/escalation-to-school pathway for school deployments not detailed on public pages; contact-risk between anonymous members managed via no-personal-info rule but not exhaustively documented. | No public DPA/data-processing agreement for institutional customers found; no explicit ICO Children's Code (Age Appropriate Design Code) reference; specific named sub-processors and any non-UK/EEA transfer safeguards for "urgent care" not fully detailed. | Minimum age stated but no description of age-verification/enforcement mechanism; suitability for under-16 (KS3) not addressed because service excludes them; no key-stage-specific content tailoring described. | No explicit full WCAG 2.2 AA conformance claim (acknowledges ongoing remediation of alt text, keyboard/structure, forms, PDFs); no VPAT/ACR published; no specific SEND or low-literacy provisions described. | No model name/version/vendor disclosure for the NLP system, no documented accuracy/false-negative posture for crisis detection, and no public auditability/independent-evaluation evidence on the technology itself.

Evidence summary: Safeguarding: Pupil-facing AI wellbeing chatbot with active safeguarding architecture: AI crisis detection for suicidal ideation/self-harm, user-initiated SOS button, helpline/emergency-contact escalation, safety-plan tool, Mailgun-sent SOS alert emails, human 'Coach' oversight, and DCB 0129 clinical-safety compliance plus a named Clinical Safety Officer. Terms state 'our system may detect any safety risks in your messages and provide contact details for urgent care support' (https://legal.wysa.io/terms) and security/governance confirms 'We also comply with DCB 0129 standards for clinical safety of our products' and a 'Clinical Safety Officer (CSO)' (https://legal.wysa.io/). Sub-processor 'Mailgun (Sinch)... Sends alert emails when users trigger SOS/self-harm alerts' (https://legal.wysa.io/wysa-service-providers). || DataPrivacy: Strong UK GDPR posture: certified 'ISO 27001 and ISO 27701' and DCB 0129 (https://legal.wysa.io/), named DPO/CISO/CSO. Clear controller/processor split: 'Where we perform personal data processing at the instruction and direction of your Institution, we will be data processors' (https://legal.wysa.io/privacy-policy). LLM training is excluded with redaction: 'Your conversation messages are never stored by the AI service provider and used by them to train their LLM' and identifiers removed via 'Private AI' before sending (https://legal.wysa.io/children/privacy-policy, https://legal.wysa.io/wysa-service-providers). Hosting on AWS; encryption in transit/at rest stated. Full named sub-processor list published (https://legal.wysa.io/wysa-service-providers). || Age: Clear, tiered age policy: app 'intended for use... by individuals aged 13+ (or 11+ if agreed by the Institution)'; the CYP service is 'available for users in the 13 to 17 age group' and 'not to be used for ages under 13 years and above 18 years' (https://legal.wysa.io/terms). Dedicated child-friendly Terms and Privacy Policy exist, parental/guardian consent is contemplated ('Wysa may need your parent or legal guardian's permission'), and content is purpose-built wellbeing support for young people (https://legal.wysa.io/children/privacy-policy). || Accessibility: Published Accessibility Statement (current as of 10 March 2025) stating the app and website are 'partially compliant with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) version 2.1 A-AA', assessed annually against Level A and AA; screen-reader support via 'TalkBack (Android)' and 'VoiceOver (iOS)', sufficient contrast ratios, captions for AV, and non-colour feedback (vibration/text) (https://www.wysa.com/accessibility-statement, verified via cached search snippets). || Transparency: Discloses its AI stack: sub-processor list names 'Open AI - LLM enabled AI alongside Wysa's own proprietary AI' and 'Private AI' for redaction (https://legal.wysa.io/wysa-service-providers). Policies are version-stamped (privacy policy v7.1.1, revised 8 January 2026; https://legal.wysa.io/) with a public change log. Vendor cites '45 peer reviewed studies' and DCB 0129 clinical-safety compliance, supporting auditability of clinical claims.
Evidence gaps: Terms also state Wysa is 'not recommended for use in crisis situations' and 'not for crises or emergencies', so it is an adjunct not a crisis service. No explicit KCSIE 2025 alignment found in any fetched document. Age/identity is self-declared ('Wysa is not responsible if someone lies about their age') creating contact-risk uncertainty. The role-of-AI-in-SOS detail page (wysa.com) returned HTTP 403 and could not be directly verified beyond search snippets. | Default retention is long: 'we keep your information for up to 10 years from the last time you updated it' (https://legal.wysa.io/privacy-policy). No explicit ICO Children's Code / Age Appropriate Design Code reference in any fetched policy. Directly-fetched global policy says data 'saved... in our cloud servers managed by AWS' with no UK-residency claim; UK-based AES-256 storage appears only in search snippets of legal.wysa.io and the UK-specific notice (legal.wysa.uk) returned HTTP 403, so UK residency could not be verified from a fetched page. A separate signable DPA document was not located. | Enforcement is weak/self-declared: 'Wysa is not responsible if someone lies about their age' (https://legal.wysa.io/terms) with no robust age-verification mechanism described. The 11+ floor depends on institutional agreement. No detailed key-stage content-appropriateness mapping found. | Conforms to WCAG 2.1 (not the requested 2.2 AA) and is only 'partially compliant'. No VPAT/ACR published. No specific SEND or low-literacy provisions described. The accessibility-statement page itself returned HTTP 403 to the fetcher, so wording was verified only via search-result extracts, not a direct fetch. | No specific LLM model name/version disclosed (only 'OpenAI'). No explicit hallucination/error posture for the generative component published in fetched docs; the generative-AI privacy page rendered only navigation, not policy content. The 'how it works' and role-of-AI-in-SOS explainer pages on wysa.com returned HTTP 403, so the depth of public technical/transparency disclosure was partly verified via search snippets rather than direct fetch.

Evidence summary: Safeguarding: Ebb AI companion has a Safety Risk Identification system monitoring 100% of messages for suicidal ideation, self-harm, abuse etc., escalating to crisis care and clinician review (https://www.headspace.com/ai). Ebb is restricted to 18+ (https://www.headspace.com/ai-mental-health-companion). Teen meditation content (13-18) is benign with parental consent for 13-17 (https://www.headspace.com/terms-and-conditions). || DataPrivacy: Headspace is 'a data controller under the GDPR for the majority of the personal information' and only a processor for limited Benefit Sponsor data; data stored in the US ('Headspace is a US based company and your personal information will be stored within the US') (https://www.headspace.com/privacy-policy). Minimum age 13. Data encrypted at rest and in transit (https://www.headspace.com/personal-security). || Age: Platform 'not intended for individuals below 13 years old'; Headspace for Teens serves 13-18 (US), with verifiable parent/guardian consent for 13-17; under-13 only via supervised parent account; AI companion Ebb is 18+ only (https://www.headspace.com/terms-and-conditions, https://www.headspace.com/teens, https://www.headspace.com/ai). 'For Educators' product is 18+ staff-only. || Accessibility: Accessibility statement targets 'WCAG Version 2.2 Level AA', partners with Accessible by Design LLC, and publishes Web App and Mobile App VPATs dated February 2026; commits to visual, auditory, cognitive and motor accessibility; feedback channel accessibility@headspace.com (https://www.headspace.com/accessibility-statement). || Transparency: Detailed AI principles disclose that Ebb uses 'multiple models...orchestrated', has guardrails against medical advice/diagnosis and out-of-scope use, an automated 'LLM-as-a-judge' safety/quality evaluation, clinician review of high-risk messages, and red-teaming/post-release tracking (https://www.headspace.com/ai). Trained on LLMs plus proprietary in-house datasets (https://www.headspace.com/ai-mental-health-companion).
Evidence gaps: No KCSIE alignment or UK safeguarding framing found. No school-facing safeguarding workflow: no DSL reporting/escalation, no school admin oversight or supervision model for pupils. Crisis routing is US-centric (988), not UK pathways (999/Shout/Childline). Pupil access is brokered via third-party US partners (Peer Health Exchange, Bring Change 2 Mind), not a school-managed system. Pupil-facing meditation content has no described real-time moderation comparable to Ebb. | No publicly available DPA for schools/organisations found; no public sub-processor list; retention criteria are vague ('advisable in light of our legal position') with deletion only 'under certain circumstances'. Controller (not processor) posture is problematic for a school as data controller. No ICO Children's Code/Age Appropriate Design alignment found. No SOC 2/ISO 27001 certification stated on the security page. UK data residency not offered (US-only storage). | 13+ floor and teen pathways are US-framed; UK key-stage appropriateness not addressed. No school-level age-gating controls described; enforcement relies on self-declared age and parental consent rather than verified mechanisms suitable for UK primary pupils. | VPAT/ACR contents not read in detail (could not confirm number/severity of partial-support items). No specific SEND or low-literacy provisions detailed; conformance described as 'to the maximum extent possible' rather than full certified conformance. | No specific underlying model names/versions disclosed; no independent third-party audit of the AI; no published hallucination-rate or accuracy metrics; auditability is internal only. Transparency relates to the AI feature (Ebb), which is 18+ and so not the pupil-facing component.

Evidence summary: Safeguarding: Calm for Schools (https://www.calm.com/schools) is the consumer human-narrated meditation/mindfulness app offered to school staff and students; free classroom audio resources require no login (https://support.calm.com/hc/en-us/articles/1260801295209). It is a one-way content library: no user-to-user contact, no chat, low contact risk. No KCSIE reference, child-safety page, moderation, reporting/escalation, or supervision model found on any fetched Calm page. || DataPrivacy: Privacy Policy (https://www.calm.com/privacy-policy) names Calm.com, Inc. as controller, includes a UK/EEA section, uses SCCs for US transfers, and retains data 'no longer than necessary'. A DPA exists (https://www.calm.com/data-processing-agreement) with SOC 2 audit access. However the consumer policy permits sharing/'selling' personal data with advertising partners for targeted ads (opt-out via calm.com/optout) — a serious concern in a children-facing education context. || Age: Calm Terms of Service (https://www.calm.com/terms) state: 'You must be 16 years or older to use the Services' (parental/guardian consent if under the age of majority). The core app's stated minimum age (16) is above the age of much KS3/KS4 and all primary cohorts, despite the schools offering targeting students. Calm Kids content exists within the app. || Accessibility: Official Accessibility Statement (https://www.calm.com/accessibility-statement) states a goal to align with WCAG 2.1 AA and EN 301 549, and a partnership with Level Access to design, develop and test digital properties for conformance; statement prepared 2023-05-04. || Transparency: Calm is a library of human-narrated audio (meditations, Sleep Stories); it does not generate meditation content with AI. AI is used for recommendation/personalisation (e.g. Amazon Personalize, LLM-enriched content descriptions) per vendor-corroborated reporting, but no model/version disclosure, hallucination posture, or 'how it works' transparency appears on Calm's own product/help pages.
Evidence gaps: No safeguarding/child-protection statement, no KCSIE 2025 alignment, no harm-reporting or escalation pathway, and no defined supervision model for pupil use were located. Could not verify any UK-education-specific safeguarding controls. www.calm.com pages returned 403 to direct fetch; relied on search-engine extracts of those pages plus support.calm.com. | No reference to the ICO Children's Code / Age Appropriate Design Code; no published sub-processor list; the DPA is framed around business 'Customer Data' (employee identifiers) rather than pupil data, with no education-specific or pupil-data terms. No statement that pupil data is excluded from advertising/targeting. www.calm.com DPA/policy pages 403'd direct fetch; relied on search extracts. | Could not verify any enforced under-16 access path appropriate to UK key stages; the 16+ terms appear to conflict with promoting student use below 16. No KS-mapped content-appropriateness documentation found. Age-gating enforcement mechanism not verified. | No published VPAT/ACR or conformance report found; language is aspirational ('goal to align', 'continues to develop') rather than a tested conformance claim. Targets WCAG 2.1 (not 2.2). No SEND/low-literacy or specific assistive-tech feature documentation verified. Statement is ~3 years old. | No vendor-published statement on the AI personalisation/recommendation system, its data inputs (mood logging), model versions, or auditability. Transparency assessed as applicable because AI personalisation is present, but Calm provides no first-party disclosure; corroboration came from third-party/AWS sources, not Calm's own policy pages.

Evidence summary: Safeguarding: Dedicated child mental-health charity; published Safeguarding & Child Protection Policy referencing 'Keeping Children Safe in Education 2025', named Head of Safeguarding (Sharon Cole), separate Child Protection Procedures and Adult Safeguarding Policy. https://www.place2be.org.uk/about-us/our-policies/safeguarding-policy/ ; https://www.place2be.org.uk/media/a0uibbpr/child-protection-policy.pdf || DataPrivacy: External Privacy Policy (controller Place2Be, contact privacy@place2be.org.uk) and a service-user 'Information Sheets and Privacy Notice' approved by the Information Governance & Security Committee Sept 2025; references UK GDPR/DPA 2018, legitimate interests and contract bases. https://www.place2be.org.uk/media/feqdurn5/p2b-external-privacy-policy-july-2023.pdf ; https://www.place2be.org.uk/media/erullrca/information-sheets-and-privacy-notice-dec-24.pdf || Age: School-based counselling spanning primary and secondary; sub-services age-banded e.g. PIPT 'especially 3 to 8-year-olds', KIT for secondary/high schools, third-party Kooth/ThinkNinja stated as age 10+. https://www.place2be.org.uk/our-services/schools/mental-health-services-for-pupils/ || Accessibility: Accessibility statement: 'We aim to ensure that all pages on our website conform to the Level AA standard of the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1'; directs users to AbilityNet and OS/browser accessibility features. https://www.place2be.org.uk/website-policies/accessibility/ || Transparency: N/A
Evidence gaps: Full reporting timescales, escalation steps, supervision model and information-sharing thresholds were in PDFs whose body text was not machine-extractable; KCSIE 2025 reference confirmed but detailed harm pathways not directly quotable. As a human-delivered service (Class B safeguarding interpretation), safeguarding is its core purpose and is strongly supported by published policy. | Could not verify specific retention periods, UK data residency/international-transfer terms, named sub-processors, or explicit ICO Age Appropriate Design Code alignment; service-user privacy notice PDF body was not machine-readable. No DPA/data-processing addendum surfaced publicly, though school T&Cs (Jan 2026) exist. No ICO registration number located. | No single enforced minimum age (human-delivered service accessed via the school, not an account-based product); content is professionally mediated and key-stage appropriate by design, so age-gating is handled by school/clinician rather than a technical control. | Aspirational ('aim to') rather than a tested conformance claim; WCAG 2.1 (not 2.2), no last-reviewed date, no VPAT/ACR, no list of known issues, and not in the UK public-sector statement format. Scope is the marketing website only — the therapeutic service itself is human-delivered with no audited digital product. | Model/version, hallucination posture and auditability are inapplicable as there is no AI system; Transparency set na.

Evidence summary: Safeguarding: Nova is explicitly "not a crisis or emergency intervention service" and "is equipped to direct users to professional help" following "protocols set by Clinical Psychologists" (https://unmind.com/feature-nova; https://unmind.com/ai-trust-and-safety). T&Cs carry a medical-emergency disclaimer: "If you think you have a medical emergency, you should call your doctor or the emergency services immediately" (https://unmind.com/terms-and-conditions). Nova built by Clinical Psychologists with a dedicated team that "manually tests the responses" (https://unmind.com/ai-trust-and-safety). || DataPrivacy: Platform "hosted in the European Union (EU) within the Republic of Ireland"; lawful bases incl. explicit consent for optional special-category/health data; account ID/contact deleted within 60 days and interaction records "permanently and irrevocably anonymized" (https://unmind.com/privacy-policy). Sub-processors named: OpenAI (US, 30-day prompt retention) and Cartesia (EU, zero retention); "Your data will never be used by OpenAI or Cartesia to train AI models" (https://unmind.com/privacy-policy). DPA available; ISO/IEC 27001 certified, Cyber Essentials certified, encryption at rest/in transit (https://trust.unmind.com/). || Age: T&Cs state "Coaching and Therapy services are not intended for users who are below the age of 18" and "All other Services are not intended for children below 16 and you should not use or access the Services... if you are below 16" (https://unmind.com/terms-and-conditions). || Accessibility: Unmind states an "accessibility-first" design approach to its registration flow and platform, with multilingual support (Nova in 60+ languages) (https://unmind.com/blog (registration redesign); https://unmind.com/feature-nova). || Transparency: Model disclosed by name: "Nova uses a tool called GPT-4o, by OpenAI"; hallucination posture stated: "AI models are known to sometimes say things as if they're true, even if they're not. We've guarded against this by telling Nova not to give fact-based responses"; "built by Unmind researchers and Clinical Psychologists"; "a dedicated team that manually tests the responses"; alignment claimed with the "WHO Guidelines of Ethics & Governance for AI in health" and the "EU AI Act" (https://unmind.com/ai-trust-and-safety). AI governance, OpenAI sub-processing and 30-day retention also disclosed (https://unmind.com/privacy-policy; https://trust.unmind.com/).
Evidence gaps: No KCSIE alignment of any kind (this is a workplace/employee product, min age 16/18, not designed for pupils). No child-safeguarding harm pathways, no DSL/escalation routing, no supervision model for under-18s, no contact-risk controls for minors. Crisis-prevention protocol detail is gated behind the Trust Center and could not be read. An open AI chat (Nova) handling distress without pupil-context safeguarding is the key risk for any education deployment. | No mention of the ICO Children's Code / Age Appropriate Design Code (consistent with it not being a children's service). Some prompt data processed in the US by OpenAI (transfer mechanism not verified on the public page). Full Information Security Whitepaper and sub-processor list are gated behind Trust Center access request. | No age-verification/enforcement mechanism described. No content mapping to UK key stages — this is a workplace product not designed for school pupils, so it is largely inappropriate for primary/secondary cohorts and only marginally relevant for sixth-form/16+ staff-adjacent use. No ICO Children's Code consideration. | No formal accessibility statement, no VPAT/ACR, and no stated WCAG 2.2 AA conformance claim could be located on the public site or Trust Center. No assistive-technology, SEND or low-literacy testing evidence. Accessibility claims are marketing-level only and unverified against any standard. | No model-version change log or independent audit/eval results published. EU AI Act / WHO alignment asserted but not independently evidenced. Marketing pages (made-with-ai) are vaguer than the dedicated AI Trust & Safety page.

Evidence summary: Safeguarding: Staff-only (Class B) dedicated safeguarding system: structured case management, automated chronologies, staff-allegation/low-level concern handling, escalation and external-agency sharing aligned to KCSIE and Working Together; unalterable audit log 'to guarantee the integrity of the data, such that it can be relied upon in court as evidence'; 2FA access control. https://www.tes.com/for-schools/myconcern ; https://www.thesafeguardingcompany.com/media/v5upe1bj/myconcern-data-sheet-part-of-tes-online.pdf || DataPrivacy: ISO 27001 certified, Cyber Essentials Plus accredited, registered with the UK ICO (data processor for customer data); hosted in UK Microsoft Azure data centres with data 'not transferred outside of the EEA'; Data Sharing and Processing Agreement available on request. https://www.thesafeguardingcompany.com/media/v5upe1bj/myconcern-data-sheet-part-of-tes-online.pdf ; https://www.myconcern.co.uk/implementing-myconcern/information-security/ || Age: Staff-only tool with no pupil login; covers safeguarding concerns across key stages (ages 5-7, 8-10, 11-13, 14-16, 17-18). Parent access only via school-created accounts. https://edtechimpact.com/products/myconcern-safeguarding-software/ ; https://www.tes.com/for-schools/myconcern || Accessibility: No accessibility statement, VPAT/ACR or WCAG conformance claim located on vendor/Tes sites. Third-party listing notes broad device support (desktop/mobile/offline) and English/Welsh, but rates accessibility features only 'moderate'. https://edtechimpact.com/products/myconcern-safeguarding-software/ || Transparency: N/A
Evidence gaps: Specifics of internal escalation/notification workflows, role-based permission granularity and retention of safeguarding records not verified from a single canonical spec; KCSIE phrasing is vendor-stated, not independently audited. | Specific retention/deletion periods, full sub-processor list and ICO Children's Code/DPIA detail not verified from a fetched primary page; DPA is 'available upon request' rather than published; no statement located explicitly ruling out secondary/AI use of pupil data (no AI stated). | No published minimum-age policy, but largely inapplicable as pupils do not log in; content-appropriateness not a pupil-facing concern. | No WCAG 2.2 AA conformance, assistive-technology testing, SEND or low-literacy provision verifiable from any primary source; device/language breadth is not an accessibility-conformance substitute. | If any AI/analytics (e.g. pattern-spotting) is later confirmed as model-driven, a Transparency review would be warranted; not evidenced as AI in current sources.

Evidence summary: Safeguarding: Class B dedicated monitoring/safeguarding system. Vendor: "The Prevent Duty, the Department for Education's Keeping Children Safe in Education (KCSiE) guidance" and the "UK Safer Internet Centre's 'appropriate monitoring' provider checklist"; keyword libraries "developed in partnership with UK Government, charities and specialist organisations", screenshot/video captures with 'who/what/where/when', anonymous student reporting, and "incident handling and reporting tools... which can be shared with external agencies". https://www.maitek.co.uk/impero-education-pro-online-safety ; product framing https://www.ativion.com/education-pro/ || DataPrivacy: DPA incorporates EU SCCs (Module 2) and UK IDTA (ICO template B1.0), governed by England & Wales law; 72-hour breach notification; annual audit rights; processing limited to service provision. GDPR notice: "data from UK-based Customers... is kept in the UK". Public named sub-processor list with countries/purposes (AWS, Microsoft, Anthropic via Bedrock, CPOMS, Wonde etc.). ISO 27001 displayed. https://www.ativion.com/legal/dpa/ ; https://www.ativion.com/legal/GDPR ; https://www.ativion.com/legal/sub-processor || Age: Children's Privacy Notice frames children as "under the age of 13" (COPPA-style) with parental-consent/deletion handling; "Ativion only collects and uses personal information collected from students for the use and benefit of the School and for no other purpose." Product designed for UK schools across key stages. https://www.ativion.com/legal/ChildrenPrivacy || Accessibility: Accessibility statement: aims to adhere to "level AA of the WCAG 2.1 guidelines" "wherever possible", scoped only to website domains "impero.com, impero.dk". https://www.impero.com/accessibility-statement || Transparency: AI features (ARIA) run on Amazon Bedrock; AWS case study discloses Amazon Nova Pro/Lite foundation models with Bedrock Guardrails to "prevent inappropriate or unauthorized AI responses". https://aws.amazon.com/solutions/case-studies/ativion-nova-case-study/ . Sub-processor list names "Anthropic PBC | AI model provider (via Amazon Bedrock)". https://www.ativion.com/legal/sub-processor
Evidence gaps: Reseller mirror used for KCSIE/Prevent/SIC quotes as the canonical imperosoftware product pages now 302-redirect to ativion.com. No published independent moderation/efficacy data or false-positive rates; case-management/escalation workflow depth not fully documented on public pages. | ICO Age Appropriate Design Code (Children's Code) not cited anywhere. Specific retention periods for captured pupil safeguarding data (screenshots/video/keyword logs) not stated publicly. ISO 27001 certificate scope/number not verified from a fetched certificate. | No UK-specific minimum age, no ICO Children's Code / Age Appropriate Design Code reference, and no stated/enforced minimum age for the monitoring product itself. As a staff-operated monitoring tool there is no pupil age-gating; content-appropriateness per key stage not documented publicly. | Statement explicitly covers only the websites, not the Education Pro software. No VPAT/ACR, no WCAG 2.2 reference, no assistive-technology or SEND/low-literacy detail, no date, and qualified ('aim to... wherever possible') language. | Model/version and hallucination posture are disclosed mainly via an AWS marketing case study and the sub-processor list, not a vendor model card or AI transparency page. ARIA is primarily a StudentKeeper feature; the public Education Pro page gives little on how its monitoring/AI decisioning works, auditability of keyword matching, or false-positive handling. Privacy policy states "your data is never used to train or improve any third-party AI model, unless otherwise agreed." (https://www.impero.com/privacy-policy)

Evidence summary: Safeguarding: Class B dedicated safeguarding/monitoring system. Aligns to KCSIE 2025 (cites 2025 update on misinformation as harm) and Prevent; OCR across all apps/devices incl. However AI is disclosed only vaguely as 'AI-enhanced trend recognition and prioritisation' / 'latest AI models' with no model/version named, no accuracy or hallucination posture, and no published audit methodology. https://www.securus-software.com/digital-monitoring-for-education/ and https://www.securus-software.com/faq/

Evidence summary: Safeguarding: Class B monitoring system.
